description: Golden recommendations that must appear on the Mitchell hero return. Used by Agent 2's recommender as a ground-truth fixture for tests and by Agent 5's evaluator as a minimum-coverage oracle. Each entry references a real rule id from src/lib/rules/index.ts and an expected dollar impact range, goal affinity, and severity.

recommendations.headline: Fix RSU double-count on Contoso 2025-05 vest lot
recommendations.rationale: Olivia's 2025-05-15 RSU vest appears in W-2 box 12 code V AND as zero-basis same-day sale lots on the Contoso broker 1099-B. Absent the basis step-up, the RSU compensation is taxed twice. This is the canonical TurboTax RSU bug and the single highest-impact finding on the return.

recommendations.headline: Apply wash sale Code W to three disallowed-loss lots
recommendations.rationale: Three 1099-B lots report wash_sale_loss_disallowed totaling $4,180 but the lot code field is null. IRS matching will bounce the return unless Schedule D adjustments pick up Code W and disallow the $4,180. This also preserves the correct basis step-up on the replacement shares.

recommendations.headline: Raise Mitchell HSA Form 8889 line 6 to the TY2025 family limit
recommendations.rationale: Form 8889 line 6 is populated with $8,300, the TY2024 family contribution limit. TY2025 raises the family limit to $8,550. Correcting the limit preserves the above-the-line deduction and avoids a future amendment.

recommendations.headline: Split Will County rental purchase price between land and building
recommendations.rationale: The Will County rental was depreciated on the full $310K purchase price without a documented land/building allocation. IRS audit positions require an explicit allocation (typically using county assessor ratios). Allocating ~25% to land reduces the depreciable basis and exposes a prior-year adjustment.

recommendations.headline: IRC §469 active participant loss fully phased out at Mitchell AGI
recommendations.rationale: Mitchell AGI of $325,850 is well above the $150K ceiling for the §469(i) $25,000 active-participant rental loss allowance. The rental loss is fully suspended and must be tracked as a passive activity loss carryforward to 2026.

recommendations.headline: SALT cap confirmation — prior $10K limit no longer applies
recommendations.rationale: TY2025 raises the SALT cap to $40,000 MFJ under the permanent TCJA extension. Mitchell's combined state + property tax of ~$23K+ now flows through uncapped, producing itemized deduction headroom that was unavailable at the prior $10K limit.

recommendations.headline: Evaluate Illinois PTET election on Blackwood Trading K-1
recommendations.rationale: Blackwood Trading Partners LP issues a K-1 and the Illinois slice is flagged ptet_election_eligible: true. Electing PTET converts the owner's Illinois tax to an entity-level deduction, effectively uncapping that portion of SALT. Coordinate with the partnership before its state deadline.

recommendations.headline: Olivia has $9,500 of remaining 401(k) elective deferral headroom
recommendations.rationale: Olivia's W-2 box 12 code D shows $14,000 of 401(k) deferrals against the TY2025 §402(g) limit of $23,500. Increasing her last two paychecks to max the deferral saves federal tax at the marginal rate and reduces TY2025 AGI.

recommendations.headline: Form 1116 required for the Faraday Emerging Markets foreign tax credit
recommendations.rationale: The Faraday 1099-DIV reports $260 of foreign tax paid. MFJ can elect the $600-or-less simplified FTC without Form 1116, so this is advisory only — Agent 2 may suppress it in favor of the election.
